title: Shoes * Foster Children - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Shoes * Foster Children 5 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Foster Children Women Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF FIRST BAPTIST CHURCH OF PRINEVILLE HOPE'S CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers clothing, linen, shoes, and grooming supplies. 450 SE Fairview Street Prineville, OR 97754 Eligibility: Foster and adoptive children 0-18 years old Hours: 1st, 2nd, 3th and 5th Thursdays of the month 9am-11am; 4th Thursday 12:30pm-2:30pm Email (541) 848-0138 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HANDS AND FEET FOSTER PARENT SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supports foster families by providing trauma-informed TBRI training to help resource parents navigate the needs of children who have experienced neglect or abuse. To ease the transition into a new home, personalized care baskets filled with essentials, clothing, and toys, accompanied by a complimentary family dinner are provided. Eligibility: Resource parents and foster children Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 519-4562 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PROJECT 48 Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides tailored bags with essential items for foster children entering care, including clothing, hair care products, and LGBTQ2S+ sensitive options. Offers a six-week program for youth aging out of foster care, featuring workshops on financial literacy, resume building, and interview preparation. 2505 SE 11th Avenue Suite 122 Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Foster youth ages 7-23 Hours: Varies Email (971) 275-7416 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SALEM ANGELS LOVE BOX PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Fosters a supportive community for foster families. This includes caregivers, children in foster care, and any biological or adopted children living in the home. Volunteers or groups are matched with local foster families based on compatibility and location. Monthly visits provide support for the parents and a sense of normalcy, stability, and confidence for the children. 3340 Commercial Street SE Suite 110 Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: Resource parents with at least one foster child in the home Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 877-6806 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WITH LOVE RESOURCE FAMILY SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides new and gently used clothing, shoes, toys, books, and supplies. Delivery is available to those living within 30 minutes of the Tigard facility. Scheduled pick-ups at the Tigard facility are available for others, Monday-Friday. There are textured hair care classes for transracial resource parents. 14555 SW 74th Avenue Tigard, OR 97224 Eligibility: Resource parents with children in foster care ages 0-6 (up until 7th birthday) and after the child's case has changed to permanency Hours: By appointment Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details FIRST BAPTIST CHURCH OF PRINEVILLE HOPE'S CLOSET PROJECT 48 PROJECT 48 SALEM ANGELS LOVE BOX PROGRAM WITH LOVE RESOURCE FAMILY SUPPORT
